recommended_paths: Revise the TODO before renewed APROVADO to make the invariant atomic, make repair safety predicates explicit, and define aggregate deletion bypass as a narrow lifecycle boundary.
exact_next_step: Record reviewer resolutions in the governing TODO using the machine-checkable resolution table or equivalent gate ledger, then extract the derived resolution packet and decide whether another bounded review pass is still required.

findings.title: Repair execute mode needs enumerated safe-delete, safe-restore, and skip predicates.
findings.rationale: D-04 recommends deleting known test-seed aggregates, restoring safe non-test rows, and skipping ambiguous rows, but the approval decision does not define mandatory safety predicates.
findings.suggested_action: Define exact execute-mode predicates before approval: tenant match, ownership/source evidence, allowed test prefixes, linked-data handling, profile type existence, single-vs-multiple soft-deleted profile behavior, and residual reporting format.

findings.title: Last-profile deletion prevention must be atomic under concurrent deletes.
findings.rationale: The package requires service-level guards for delete and forceDelete but does not require transaction or locking semantics. Two direct profile deletes can both observe another active profile and then both delete, leaving a live account with zero active profiles.
findings.suggested_action: Add an explicit atomicity requirement for the guard, such as locking the account/profile set or an equivalent transactional invariant, and require a concurrency regression test covering simultaneous profile deletion.

findings.title: Invariant checks and repair scans must be bounded by indexed predicates.
findings.rationale: The plan does not state query-shape or index expectations for the guard or repair enumeration.
findings.suggested_action: Require bounded indexed lookups for account_id/deleted_at-style predicates and include query/log evidence from focused tests or dry-run repair.

findings.title: Aggregate account deletion bypass must be a named lifecycle boundary.
findings.rationale: The plan preserves aggregate account deletion and may need a bypass context, but an implicit or broadly callable bypass could reintroduce direct profile deletion as an invariant escape hatch.
findings.suggested_action: State that profile deletion may bypass the last-profile guard only inside the account aggregate deletion transaction/service path, and require a regression test proving direct callers cannot use that bypass.
